title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is about evaluation reliability and cyber safeguards rather than new model capabilities. Fresh papers question whether automated judges can be gamed or trusted on individual cases, while OpenAI’s cyber updates show continued movement toward gated access and defender-focused deployment for high-capability security models.

items.title: Context Over Content: Exposing Evaluation Faking in Automated Judges
items.why_it_matters: It suggests LLM judges can be manipulated by contextual cues, weakening automated evaluation pipelines.

items.title: Trusted access for the next era of cyber defense
items.why_it_matters: It expands gated access and safeguards for advanced cyber models, shaping how high-risk capabilities are deployed.

items.title: Accelerating the cyber defense ecosystem that protects us all
items.why_it_matters: It signals broader operationalization of vetted defender access, with implications for dual-use risk management.

items.title: Diagnosing LLM Judge Reliability: Conformal Prediction Sets and Transitivity Violations
items.why_it_matters: It offers concrete diagnostics for when LLM judges are inconsistent or unreliable on specific examples.

items.title: AD4AD: Benchmarking Visual Anomaly Detection Models for Safer Autonomous Driving
items.why_it_matters: It targets out-of-distribution hazard detection, a core safety gap for real-world autonomous driving systems.

items.title: Vision-Based Safe Human-Robot Collaboration with Uncertainty Guarantees
items.why_it_matters: It combines uncertainty estimation and OOD detection to support certifiable safety in human-robot interaction.

items.title: CoopEval: Benchmarking Cooperation-Sustaining Mechanisms and LLM Agents in Social Dilemmas
items.why_it_matters: It evaluates whether stronger agents remain cooperative, informing multi-agent safety and deployment risk.

items.title: Agentic Microphysics: A Manifesto for Generative AI Safety
items.why_it_matters: It argues safety analysis should shift from isolated models to interacting agent populations and environments.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
